peculiarities:
அ is the first letter of the Tamil alphabet and its most fundamental sound: the short 'a', as in 'among'. It is the most common vowel in Tamil. Tamil tradition holds அ as the source from which all letters flow. The very first Tirukkural verse begins 'அகர முதல எழுத்தெல்லாம்', meaning 'As அ is the first of all letters, so is God the first of all the world.'

Look at the shape: a spiral that opens, a stem that rises. Our snake coils and then lifts its head to speak the very first sound.
vallalar_note: For Vallalar, the first sound is not merely a letter. It is an opening. அ begins அருட்பெரும்ஜோதி (Arut Perum Jyoti), the Vast Grace-Light he sang of. Every alphabet, every prayer, every act of compassion begins the way this letter begins: with something opening toward the light.
